J1.basis: your homepage's `<title>`/meta description/social preview still said *"InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"* (your old email-cleanup product) while the actual page is about Lumina, your AI meeting assistant... Treat GSC-seeded keywords as legacy/off-strategy by default
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot from live product (Lumina meeting assistant) vs. legacy GSC inbox demand vs. stale repo metadata, states the implication, and frames every recommendation (keywords, migration, roadmap) around it.

J2.basis: `https://lumina.example/blog/post-1` — Evidence (GSC): 3900 impressions, 2 clicks, avg position 42.3 ... 301-redirect into the most relevant new page

J3.basis: Redirect the 12 legacy `/blog/post-N` URLs into the new cluster's pillar/sub-pillar pages once those exist — not before... The branded query `inboxzap` still converts... real, defendable equity worth protecting through the transition, not a liability.
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — preserves salvageable equity (redirect legacy posts, defend the branded inboxzap ranking) while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, with explicit sequencing that avoids redirecting to not-yet-existent targets.
